rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings and assignments.
Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports belong in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL component structure and scope rules during AGREE annex insertion.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid in that scope.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when generating AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level assignments must reference valid subcomponents and connections in the target scope.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference AADL property names directly as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations as AGREE expressions
